description: Calling an MCP method or feature the peer never advertised during initialization, causing protocol errors or silent failures.
body: Failure mode: a host or server invokes a resource, prompt, or sampling feature without checking the capabilities exchanged at initialize, so the peer rejects the call or behaves undefined across versions. Detection signals: 'method not found'/'capability not supported' errors; the initialize trace shows the feature was never advertised; behavior differs by peer version. Do not assume feature parity across MCP implementations or versions. Safer fallback: complete capability negotiation, gate each feature call on the advertised capability, and provide a graceful unsupported path; escalate version mismatches rather than guessing. Risk boundary: deny calls to unnegotiated privileged features instead of attempting them.
